summary: Ukrainian forces destroyed a Russian Ka-52 attack helicopter on the Pokrovsk front using an FPV drone, marking a rare instance of a helicopter being downed by this type of drone. A separate FPV drone strike damaged a Ukrainian BUK air defense system near Bolshoy Sokolny Island. Ukrainian FPV pilots are experimenting with tactics such as throwing debris at Russian positions before striking, and in one case, a Russian soldier was killed after charging at an FPV drone with a bayonet. Meanwhile, NATO has launched new test ranges in Latvia for drones and counter-drone systems, and Ukraine and Britain are developing advanced long-range maritime drones capable of open-ocean operations. Analysts note that while digital video transmission for FPV drones offers clearer imagery, analog systems remain preferred due to reliability under interference.
